executiveSummary: Transitioning the workspace component dependency graphs from the legacy MongoDB collections to our new PostgreSQL 16 instances leveraging Recursive CTEs, enabling complex graph traversal for the Agentic Orchestration harness.

architectureStates.currentStateDescription: FastAPI services query MongoDB directly. Hierarchical resolutions require multiple round-trips over the network to stitch the graph together in application memory.
architectureStates.targetStateDescription: FastAPI services query PostgreSQL 16. The database engine resolves the hierarchical trees natively using the `ltree` extension and recursive queries, returning a flattened, execution-ready array.

validationStrategy: AWS DMS Validation confirms 100% row-level parity
Shadow reads: A shadow script queries both databases for 10,000 random root nodes and diffs the output arrays

breakingChanges: Document _id fields (ObjectIDs) are mapping to UUIDv4
backwardCompatibility.mitigationForBreakingChanges: A temporary API Gateway translation layer will intercept v1 traffic, map the incoming ObjectIDs to the new UUIDs via a lookup table, and route to the new endpoints.

preRequisites: AWS DMS CDC task must report replication lag under 1 second
Shadow read diffs must return 0% errors for 72 consecutive hours

executionSteps.action: Enable dual-writes from the application layer as the primary source of truth, demoting DMS to a fallback.
Update Route53/API Gateway weights to shift 10% of read traffic to the PostgreSQL service.
Monitor Datadog metrics for 1 hour. If p99 latency is stable, shift remaining 90% of traffic.

rollbackStrategy.pointOfNoReturn: Step 3 (Traffic shift to 100%). After this point, writes occurring natively in Postgres will require a reverse-ETL script to bring Mongo back into parity.
automaticTriggers: PostgreSQL CPU utilization spikes above 90% during the 10% traffic canary phase
5xx error rates exceed 1% on the new endpoints
manualSteps: Disable the dual-write feature flag in LaunchDarkly
rollbackStrategy.dataReconciliation: If reverse-reconciliation is needed, execute the `pg-to-mongo-sync.py` script stored in the runbook repository to backfill any stranded writes.

milestones.action: Remove the API Gateway Translation Layer (forcing all clients to v2 endpoints)
Terminate MongoDB Atlas instances and delete associated VPC peering connections
